title: JSSDF vs JSDF
wikitext:
[]: JSSDF vs JSDF             Quote Post by V » Fri Nov 02, 2007 6:26 pm As I understand it, the "JSSDF" *is* Japan's new military. The old JSDF was retired/absorbed into UN forces. The JSSDF is the new version of "Japan's national military" with no Article 9 restrictions. The JASDF is a subdivision of the JSDF, as seen in Episode 10. Any evidence otherwise?

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Fri Nov 02, 2007 6:43 pm V wrote: As I understand it, the "JSSDF" *is* Japan's new military.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Fri Nov 02, 2007 6:45 pm well you see I don't think that, because the Red Cross Book entry for JSSDF said that the regular JSDF had been disolved and doesn't exist at all anymore; the JSSDF isn't like the Marine Corps, but it is the entire conventional army of 2015 Japan

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Fri Nov 02, 2007 6:49 pm V wrote: well you see I don't think that, because the Red Cross Book entry for JSSDF said that the regular JSDF had been disolved and doesn't exist at all anymore;

[]:             Quote Post by V » Fri Nov 02, 2007 6:57 pm when is a GSDF ever mentioned? As for the JASDF, they seem to be a sub-branch of the JSSDF from the Red Cross Book translation A military organization under the direct command of Japan's Defense Ministry. Triggered by military conflict between China and Vietnam over the Spratleys in 2003, it was formed by the Japanese government which had no military force of its own (the ground, naval and air divisions of the original Japan Self Defense Force had all been incorporated into the UN Military).

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Fri Nov 02, 2007 7:05 pm V wrote: when is a GSDF ever mentioned?

[]:             Quote Post by V » Fri Nov 02, 2007 7:15 pm The GSDF are the regular army types, as seen from episode one onwards.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Fri Nov 02, 2007 8:40 pm And they *didn't* just use "handguns", they were armed to the teeth with HK G11's and P90's, and flamethrowers, and RPG's...and that's not counting the VTOL Hoverjets they sent in, and the *full tank batallions*. They threw everything but the kitchen sink at Nerv HQ.

[]:             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Fri Nov 02, 2007 8:53 pm BobBQ wrote: V wrote: when is a GSDF ever mentioned?

[]:             Quote Post by V » Fri Nov 02, 2007 9:05 pm UrsusArctos wrote: And they *didn't* just use "handguns", they were armed to the teeth with HK G11's and P90's, and flamethrowers, and RPG's...and that's not counting the VTOL Hoverjets they sent in, and the *full tank batallions*. They threw everything but the kitchen sink at Nerv HQ.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Nov 02, 2007 9:25 pm BobBQ wrote: I think the UN unity thing is overblown. While every single country on the planet might conceivably join that party, I very much doubt many of them - especially the big ones - would be such suckers as to hand over the keys of the car to an organization with a decidedly lackluster track record.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Fri Nov 02, 2007 9:53 pm V wrote: Please point out when we actually see the word "GSDF" printed on something which appears in episode 01 (like "on a tank right after Sachiel does such and such...")

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Fri Nov 02, 2007 10:29 pm IRL (In Real Life for all you n00bs) basically, Japan does not have any nukes and they don't have any long range STS (surface-to-surface) missiles. They also are not allowed by their constitution to maintain a military force that could strike beyond its shores, so things like large weapons caches, a marine corps, long-range aircraft involved in logistical support (recon planes, mid-air refueling, troops transport, etc.), bombers, fighters, battleships and aircraft carriers are not apart of the JSDF arsenal. The JSDF cannot engage in combat beyond its borders either. However, amending their constitution and establishing the JSDF into "the J S SDF" is still possible. And there has been talk of pac rim nations like Australia and South Korea buddying up with Japan in a NATO-like military pact in the coming years in hopes of "stickin' it" to North Korea, China and Russia.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Fri Nov 02, 2007 10:38 pm Reichu wrote: BobBQ wrote: I think the UN unity thing is overblown. While every single country on the planet might conceivably join that party, I very much doubt many of them - especially the big ones - would be such suckers as to hand over the keys of the car to an organization with a decidedly lackluster track record.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Nov 02, 2007 10:54 pm V wrote: 1-how canonical should I consider this for our purposes Reichu?

[]:             Quote Post by V » Fri Nov 02, 2007 10:55 pm I will draw a crude childish diagram I want to get this sorted out, and I'm more concerned with finding what is true than being right here; I really wish they had explained this more clearly... Okay here's my childish, poorly drawn diagrams: This is I think what BobBQ is saying: This is what I believe: JSSDF has an article in Chronicle with lots of words -- though there's at least a nifty diagram showing how the various parties are interconnected. It says both the SDF and the SSDF answer to the Japanese government, and that the SDF dispatches the UN army, which is in turn supervised by the UN itself.

[]:             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Sat Nov 03, 2007 2:43 am THE Hal E. Burton 9000 wrote: IRL (In Real Life for all you n00bs) basically, Japan does not have any nukes and they don't have any long range STS (surface-to-surface) missiles. They also are not allowed by their constitution to maintain a military force that could strike beyond its shores, so things like large weapons caches, a marine corps, long-range aircraft involved in logistical support ( recon planes, mid-air refueling, troops transport, etc.), bombers, fighters , battleships and aircraft carriers are not apart of the JSDF arsenal. The JSDF cannot engage in combat beyond its borders either.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Sat Nov 03, 2007 9:08 am V wrote: I am calling into question your belief that the "JSSDF" is "special forces", as I believe it is the name of the overall military, of which the JASDF is a branch.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sat Nov 03, 2007 11:26 am Watch them in action, much? They rely heavily on stealth and rapid advance tactics, carry minimal identifying marks, are absolutely dependent on rigid unit discipline and, as I keep pointing out, operate their own land and air hardware rather than rely on the regular grunts. They're obviously not in the same class as the UN / GSDF rank and file.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Sat Nov 03, 2007 11:39 am V wrote: The JSSDF does not rely on "stealth" that much; they have tanks and hoverjets and such.

The JSSDF is the new version of "Japan's national military" with no Article 9 restrictions. The JASDF is a subdivision of the JSDF, as seen in Episode 10. Any evidence otherwise?
